memory.summary: I have the afternoon free, but my term project is critically behind schedule. I will skip leisure and tackle the quick chore to maintain momentum before deep work.

desires: Secure the 'quick win' of finishing the trivial chore before 22:00 to clear my mental stack and validate my discipline, avoiding the feeling of leaving loose ends.
Maintain momentum on the term project without burning out; I need to convert this afternoon's free time into tangible progress so I don't feel the crushing weight of the Thursday deadline tomorrow.
Avoid any friction or awkward explanations to my close friend group by ensuring my schedule looks intentional and productive rather than chaotic or avoidant.

goals.description: Execute 'quick_chore' immediately to clear the mental stack and validate discipline before the 22:00 cutoff.
goals.rationale: Achievement value drives the need for closure on loose ends; avoiding friction with myself by not leaving tasks undone is paramount.

goals.description: Draft a structured, non-committal response to the close friend group explaining the afternoon's focus on coursework to maintain an intentional image.
goals.rationale: Avoidant conflict style dictates smoothing over potential perceptions of chaos; deliberate planning ensures this message protects social harmony without requiring deep emotional vulnerability.

goals.description: Allocate remaining afternoon hours to term project milestones, prioritizing low-friction progress over deep creative leaps.
goals.rationale: While the Thursday deadline creates pressure, my current energy (6) and procrastination bias (2) suggest deferring heavy cognitive load until after the immediate dopamine hit of the chore is secured.

agent.last_reasoning: Clearing the trivial chore validates discipline before the 22:00 cutoff, satisfying achievement drives. I skip leisure now to avoid friction with my own standards, though I will text cohort_3 later to explain this focus.
